payload.text:
label the send prompt button as "Send prompt" and title: "ALT+S". label Remove button as "Remove".
label start new chat button as "Start New chat" and title: "CTRL+J".
ensure: very professional codes. no errors.
if the url understanding is enabled, change the button title to "Disable URL understanding" and if not enabled the title is remained "Enable URL understanding". ensure: very dynamic.

**Defining interface elements**

I've been focusing on interface design. The "Send" button is now labeled "Send prompt" with the title "ALT+S". Also, I've renamed the attachment remove button to "Remove". Finally, the button for starting a new chat has become "Start New chat" with a title of "CTRL+J".


**Refining UX Dynamics**

I'm now implementing the dynamic behavior for the "URL Understanding" toggle. Specifically, I'm updating the button title in `components/ChatInput.tsx` based on the enabled state. It will alternate between "Disable URL understanding" and "Enable URL understanding." I've also updated the "Start New chat" button in `App.tsx` and the "Remove" button in `components/ChatInput.tsx`. I am aiming for professional UX through Tailwind CSS and Lucide icons.


**Updating Button Text & Titles**

I'm now implementing the label updates. The "Send" button will now show "Send prompt" with the title "ALT+S", just as planned. Also, the "Remove" buttons will have visible "Remove" text. Finally, the "Start New chat" button now displays that text, with the title "CTRL+J".

entries.description: Update 'New Chat' button label to 'Start New chat' and add 'CTRL+J' title for accessibility and clarity.
Update Send button to 'Send prompt' with 'ALT+S' title, add 'Remove' labels to attachment removal buttons, and dynamic titles for URL Understanding.

payload.text:
change the api key pool url to: https://mainsite-kcvz.onrender.com/tafb/key_pool.json?etag=1&n=2&client=key&maxage=1200
add a start voice input button below the input box "Alt+d" to toggle start or off.
when starts voice input, record with modern and highest technology.
while recording, set btn text to "Stop Voice Input" which stops recording and sends a request to pool api after retreiving keys.
prompt: "Transcribe the uploaded audio in the original audio language accurately. Never type anything except the transcribed text.".
when api returns a success, re request the api with the returned transcribed text as a prompt as regular.
ensure: very professional and no errors.
correctly send the recorded audio to files api like other files.
now, build.

**Implementing Voice Input**

I've begun by tackling the API key pool URL update. My initial focus is modifying the `KEY_POOL_URL` within `services/api.ts` to `https://mainsite-kcvz.on`. This is the first step towards enabling the voice input feature, as the API key pool is a critical dependency.


**Adding Voice Input Button**

I'm now focusing on the UI/UX aspects of the voice input feature. I plan to add a button below the input box within `ChatInput.tsx`. I've decided on "Alt+D" as the toggle shortcut, with the button text changing from "Start Voice Input" to "Stop Voice Input" to indicate the active state. My immediate goal is a seamless user experience.


**Defining Transcription Workflow**

I'm now clarifying the process for voice input. The user's voice generates an audio blob, uploaded for transcription by Gemini, and the returned text will trigger the standard `handleSendMessage` function, exactly as if typed. I'm structuring this as a "speak to text" prompt. I've broken it down into upload audio, transcribe, and call `handleSendMessage(transcribedText)`. I'm starting to assess changes needed in `services/api.ts`, and `components/ChatInput.tsx`, and whether `App.tsx` needs any modifications.


**Refining Implementation Strategy**

I'm now contemplating the most suitable location for the "transcribe -> send message" logic. I'm leaning toward incorporating it within `ChatInput`, as it naturally handles user input, though this requires integrating API transcription functionality. My immediate task is to decide if I need a separate `transcribeAudio` function, or if modifying `streamGeminiResponse` to decouple it from chat history suffices. I'm focusing on the stateless nature of transcription to guide this decision.


**Defining Transcription Steps**

I'm now focusing on a new function, `transcribeAudio`, which will handle key retrieval, file upload (using an existing function), and calling `generateContent` with a system instruction. The immediate focus is on the implementation within `components/ChatInput.tsx`. I'll introduce state for `isRecording` and `isTranscribing`, along with refs for `MediaRecorder` and audio chunks. The `startRecording` and `stopRecording` functions will manage the recording flow, leading to blob conversion and a call to `transcribeAudio` within the `onstop` callback. I'll integrate a mic button within the UI alongside event handling for "Alt+D".


**Prioritizing API Integration**

I'm now integrating the transcription with the existing API flow. The plan involves a two-step process: First, transcribing audio; second, resending the transcription to the API as a standard message. I'm focusing on defining `transcribeAudio` function within `services/api.ts` and UI updates in `ChatInput.tsx` to handle the recording and user interface elements. My immediate focus is on updating `services/api.ts`, and also `components/ChatInput.tsx` with a Mic icon. I'm also confirming what mimeType should be supported (audio/webm or audio/mp4). I need to determine how to handle edge cases like microphone permission issues.


**Finalizing Gemini Transcription**

I've just added the `transcribeAudio` function, which now retrieves keys, uploads audio files, and uses GoogleGenAI to transcribe the audio into text using `gemini-2.5-flash`. The function handles the API interaction effectively, ensuring accurate transcription. I am handling errors and key management. The mimeType is dynamically passed to the model, ensuring broader compatibility. The result will be used in a `handleSendMessage` call.


**Refactoring Audio Transcription**

I realized `uploadFileToGemini` within `services/api.ts` needs no modification. Now, I'm integrating the `transcribeAudio` function into `ChatInput.tsx`. I've added a mic icon, state for recording and transcribing, refs for `MediaRecorder` and audio chunks, and a `handleVoiceToggle` function with an "Alt+D" keyboard listener. My next step is to integrate the recording logic.


**Implementing Recording Logic**

I'm now integrating the recording logic. I'm adding `startRecording` and `stopRecording` functions within `ChatInput.tsx` to handle microphone interaction. The `startRecording` function uses `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia` to access the microphone and sets up the `MediaRecorder`. It also handles data and stop events, creating an audio blob for transcription. Upon stopping, I will set `isTranscribing` and call `transcribeAudio`, after cleaning up the stream.


**Enhancing UI Feedback**

I'm now incorporating UX improvements. I've focused on visual cues to confirm the recording state, showing "Stop Voice Input" while active, and implementing a loading indicator to show transcription in progress. I'm prioritizing providing clear feedback to the user on the status of their voice input.


**Verifying Audio Flow**

I'm now ensuring the transcription workflow is correctly structured. I've realized `transcribeAudio` within `services/api.ts` must return the transcribed text, as the application calls `onSendMessage` with this text. I'll also add `transcribeAudio` in `services/api.ts`, and make `uploadFileToGemini` public. I'm verifying imports and the API structure, and will consider disabling other inputs during recording.


**Refining Key Fetch**

I'm now focusing on how `transcribeAudio` should get the API key. Since `uploadFileToGemini` takes `apiKey`, I'll need to fetch the key within `transcribeAudio` and pass it to that existing function. The plan is to ensure the function works effectively in the new flow.


**Defining XML Preparation**

I'm now focusing on preparing the XML and ensuring the voice input process works as intended. I've realized the `uploadFileToGemini` function, which is defined within `services/api.ts`, is not exported. I'll define `transcribeAudio` in the same file to grant it access. I'll pass the `transcribedText` to `onSendMessage` with a `file=null`, `links=[]`, and keep the existing `isUrlUnderstanding` state. I'm now certain the system should treat the transcribed text immediately as a standard prompt.


**Integrating `onSendMessage`**

I'm now integrating the `transcribeAudio` within `services/api.ts` so that it calls `handleSendMessage` in `ChatInput.tsx`. I'll pass the `transcribedText` and ensure `file`, `links`, and `isUrlUnderstanding` are correctly handled as in the existing standard text input. I'm focusing on ensuring the API calls work with the existing functionality.



**Verifying Text Extraction**

I'm now confirming the text extraction method within `transcribeAudio`, ensuring I adhere to the updated guidelines. The previous implementation used `response.response.text()`, which is deprecated. I must now replace this with `response.text` for correct handling of `GenerateContentResponse`. My focus is on ensuring the transcribed text is properly returned for use by `handleSendMessage`.


**Implementing UI Integration**

I'm now integrating the visual elements within `ChatInput.tsx`. I've added the mic icon and a stop circle, setting up the button's UI state. I'm handling the recording/transcribing states and implementing the "Alt+D" toggle. The button will change dynamically to show "Stop Voice Input" when recording and "Transcribing..." when transcribing. My goal is a polished user experience.



**Finalizing User Experience**

I'm now putting the finishing touches on the voice input feature. I've focused on integrating the `transcribeAudio` within `services/api.ts` so that it calls `handleSendMessage` in `ChatInput.tsx`. The goal is to fully address the requirement of passing the transcribed text. I'll pass this as the message to the `handleSendMessage` function. I am also making sure `file`, `links`, and `isUrlUnderstanding` are handled as with standard text input.


**Refining Key Usage**

I'm now revisiting API key retrieval within `transcribeAudio`, given the shift towards dynamic key management. I'll fetch and manage keys within the new `transcribeAudio` function itself to ensure consistency with the new architecture. My immediate concern is securing the API key, and I will ensure that it is used within the function scope.


**Finalizing Microphone Integration**

I'm now putting the finishing touches on the voice input functionality. I've ensured the mic icon displays correctly in `ChatInput.tsx`, and that it now triggers the recording. The transcription now handles the mic icon and ensures that the stop icon appears. I have made sure to fetch API keys dynamically to securely handle the transcription within the existing flow.


**Implementing Audio Transcription**

I'm now integrating the provided file upload and Gemini transcription logic. I've focused on adapting the existing `uploadFileToGemini` function for API key retrieval, and I'm updating it to include the text extraction process for the audio transcription. My current focus is integrating the complete response process from the provided code into `transcribeAudio` within `services/api.ts`.


**Integrating Transcription Results**

I'm now integrating the updated transcription code within `transcribeAudio` to manage responses and error handling. I'm focusing on parsing the `GenerateContentResponse` correctly, ensuring the transcribed text is returned, and incorporating thorough error logging. I will call `handleSendMessage` with the extracted text.


**Refining Error Handling**

I'm now integrating robust error handling within `transcribeAudio`, specifically focusing on how API key failures are managed. I'm implementing a retry mechanism with fallback keys within `transcribeAudio` to avoid failed transcriptions. The immediate goal is to gracefully handle key exhaustion and provide informative error messages.


**Refining Data Integration**

I'm now integrating the provided code, adapting how the YouTube links and URL understanding interact with the Gemini API. Specifically, I am updating the `generateContentStream` method within `services/api.ts` to manage this integration. I'll make sure to handle video processing within the response stream, including `mimeType` handling, and integrating grounding metadata into the new workflow.


**Refining Stream Integration**

I'm now integrating the provided code, adapting stream logic within `generateContentStream` in `services/api.ts` to include `mimeType` handling and grounding metadata. Specifically, I'm refactoring the streaming content within the method to gracefully process and include the necessary metadata. I'm focusing on handling both video content and text.

I'm now integrating the provided `ChatInput` props and state with the voice input functionality. I'm focusing on ensuring the new transcription flow integrates with the existing text input, file handling, and YouTube link features. I'll pass the `isRecording` and `isTranscribing` states, the text, and file appropriately. I'm also preparing for the on-message method.


**Implementing UI State**

I'm now integrating the provided code, specifically focusing on UI interaction and message handling within `ChatInput`. I've added refs and `useImperativeHandle` for `textarea`, and `fileInput`. My focus is on integrating the state management correctly. I will add `useEffect` to manage text prefilling, and the `handleSubmit` event that handles submissions. I'll test text input, file handling, and YT links.

I've just integrated the `startRecording` and `stopRecording` functionality within `ChatInput`. The recording manages audio chunks with `chunksRef`, and the `stopRecording` function creates a `Blob` before passing it to `transcribeAudio`, which triggers `onSendMessage`. Error handling ensures transcription failures are reported, and cleaning up the stream after a stop.

**Refining Error Handling**

I'm now integrating robust error handling within `stopRecording`. I will also implement a better fallback to avoid failing on failed transcriptions. The immediate goal is to gracefully handle key exhaustion and other microphone issues.

entries.description: Update KEY_POOL_URL and add transcribeAudio function.
